Scenario.title: Indian Critical Infrastructure Intrusion on Communication Networks
Scenario.overview: Communications Industry Complex A sophisticated multi-stage attack targeting communication infrastructure, leveraging spearphishing, credential theft, and network manipulation to disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Logon Session, Script, Target System Artifact, Command, Open Source Intelligence (OSINT), Process, Domain Name Registrar, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Logon Session Creation, Script Execution, Exploit TEE Vulnerability, Command Execution, Manipulate Device Communication, Process Creation, Pass the Hash, Reg.exe Utility, Network Traffic Content

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker collects email headers and content by manipulating logon sessions to gather information on communication infrastructure vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: Specialized scripts are developed to exploit communication protocols, enabling tailored attacks on network systems.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: A spearphishing campaign delivers malicious links to compromise specific personnel, gaining initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-67: String Format Overflow in syslog()
stage.description: Unpatched vulnerabilities in communication protocols are exploited to execute arbitrary code on target systems.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-516: Hardware Component Substitution During Baselining
stage.description: Malicious bundles are deployed to maintain persistent access to critical infrastructure systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Cryptographic techniques are used to hide C2 traffic, evading network monitoring tools.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Malicious deployment tools steal credentials from processes to enable lateral movement.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Network sniffing tools map internal communication network topology to identify key targets.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: Stolen credentials are used to move laterally across the network, compromising additional systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Automated commands extract sensitive communication metadata for exfiltration.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Process creation techniques tunnel data out of the network undetected.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Network traffic is manipulated to disable critical communication hardware, creating operational chaos.

Scenario.summary: A complex attack chain targeting communication infrastructure begins with reconnaissance and resource development, followed by spearphishing for initial access. Exploits and persistence mechanisms establish control, while defense evasion and credential access facilitate lateral movement. Sensitive data is collected and exfiltrated before disrupting network operations through denial-of-service attacks.
